$comment: c90 (D-CSUBSET-ASSIGN-VALUE-RHS-COERCE, 2026-07-02): a plain assignment used as a VALUE (comma operand / chained / condition / operand) coerces the RHS to the LVALUE's type before the store (C 6.5.16p3). Pre-fix BOTH HIR value-position arms (finishAssign + lowerBinary Assign) stored at the RHS's width (the c74 compound arms coerce; statement/init assigns coerce — exactly plain-`=`-as-value was missed): sub-int->wider left the wider slot's upper bits STALE (the sqlite detonation: estimateTableWidth's `for(i=pTab->nCol, ...)` i16->int comma assign made i=0x008c0005 instead of 5 -> a 3822-element overrun -> the SQL SIGSEGV = the c90 wall); wider->sub-int stored full width CLOBBERING neighbors (real corruption); chains compounded. FIX: one coerce per arm, mirroring the statement path. 7 checks, distinct exits (the exact sqlite struct-walk shape, neighbor-integrity both directions, chains, double->int via a volatile source, the raw stale-upper witness) with dirty() stack-junk pre-fill so zero-luck cannot mask. RED-ON-DISABLE: checks 1/2/3/5/6/7 fail. => 42.
